bouquet_core_missing.cause: The bouquets.tv, bouquets.radio index or a valid lamedb file could not be found.
bouquet_core_missing.consequences: The channel list may be empty, incomplete or impossible to reload.
bouquet_core_missing.restart: Restart the Enigma2 GUI after restoring the files.
steps: Do not run an automatic repair unless you have a backup of /etc/enigma2.
Restore the most recent known-good channel-list backup.
Make sure the files were extracted directly into /etc/enigma2.
Reload the channel list or restart the GUI after restoring the files.
bouquet_core_missing.title: Main channel-list files are missing

bouquet_missing_refs.cause: bouquets.tv or bouquets.radio points to userbouquet files that do not exist in /etc/enigma2.
bouquet_missing_refs.consequences: The channel-list menu may contain empty entries, errors or an incomplete list.
bouquet_missing_refs.restart: A GUI restart is not required if the channel-list reload completes successfully.
steps: Create a backup of the bouquet index files.
Remove only lines that refer to files that do not exist.
Do not delete existing userbouquet files.
Reload the channel list after the repair.
bouquet_missing_refs.title: The index contains references to missing bouquets

crash_generic.cause: The log contains a crash traceback, but E2 Doctor could not identify a single clear problem category.
crash_generic.consequences: The problem may occur again when the same action is repeated.
steps: Check the indicated location: {file}, line {line}, function {function}.
Write down the exact actions performed immediately before the crash.
Review the last traceback lines and the plug-in name.
Update the suspected plug-in and the Enigma2 image.
Send the complete crashlog to the developer together with the receiver model and image version.
crash_generic.title: An Enigma2 crashlog was detected

crash_import.cause: A plug-in refers to a missing or incompatible function, class or library.
crash_import.consequences: This usually means that the plug-in is incompatible with the installed image or Python version.
crash_import.restart: Restart the GUI after changing or updating the plug-in.
steps: Check the indicated location: {file}, line {line}, function {function}.
Check the plug-in name shown in the traceback: {plugin}.
Update the plug-in to a version compatible with the installed image.
If the error appeared after an image update, remove the old plug-in version and install the current one.
Do not copy individual libraries from another image.
crash_import.title: Plug-in import error

crash_network.cause: The receiver has no active route to the internet or its network interface is not working.
crash_network.consequences: Plug-ins will not be able to download data, lists, updates or EPG information.
steps: Check the IP address, gateway and DNS settings.
Check the wired or Wi-Fi connection.
Run the E2 Doctor network test.
Restart the router only if other devices are affected as well.
crash_network.title: The network is unavailable

crash_no_space.cause: A process could not write a file because the partition is full or no free inodes remain.
crash_no_space.consequences: Settings, updates, logs and plug-in data may not be saved.
crash_no_space.restart: Restart the GUI after freeing space.
steps: Do not perform further installations or updates.
Check free space and inodes with df -h and df -i.
Find the largest files in the system flash.
Remove old logs and uninstall unused packages.
crash_no_space.title: No space left on device

crash_permission.cause: A process tried to read, write or execute a file without the required permissions.
crash_permission.consequences: The plug-in function cannot complete its operation.
steps: Check the path shown in the crashlog.
Compare the owner and permissions with other files of the same type.
Do not apply chmod 777 to entire system directories.
Scripts and executable files normally require execute permission.
crash_permission.title: Permission denied for a file or directory

crash_python_error.cause: The crashlog contains the error “{error}”. It is usually caused by an incompatible plug-in version, invalid data or a programming error.
crash_python_error.consequences: Opening the same screen or function may crash Enigma2 again.
crash_python_error.restart: Restart the GUI after updating or removing the faulty plug-in.
steps: Check the indicated location: {file}, line {line}, function {function}.
Check the plug-in named in the traceback: {plugin}.
Update it to a version compatible with the installed image and Python version.
If the problem appeared after changing its configuration, restore the last known-good plug-in settings.
Send the complete crashlog and a description of the actions leading to the error to the plug-in developer.
crash_python_error.title: Python code error in a plug-in

crash_python_module.cause: The code tries to import the module “{module}”, but it is not installed in the system.
crash_python_module.consequences: The plug-in shown in the crashlog will not start until a compatible dependency is installed.
crash_python_module.restart: Restart the GUI after installing the dependency.
steps: Check the indicated location: {file}, line {line}, function {function}.
Check which plug-in is named in the traceback: {plugin}.
Search the image feed for a package providing the module “{module}”.
Do not install packages with pip into the system Python when the image provides an OPKG package.
Install the dependency only if it is intended for the Python version used by the image.
crash_python_module.title: A Python module is missing

crash_readonly.cause: The kernel may have remounted a partition read-only after detecting filesystem errors.
crash_readonly.consequences: The system cannot save settings or install packages.
crash_readonly.restart: Perform a full receiver restart after repairing the filesystem.
steps: Do not force writes before reviewing the kernel log.
Check mount and dmesg | tail -n 100.
Run a filesystem check from another system or service mode.
If the problem affects internal flash and returns, back up your data and reflash the image.
crash_readonly.title: The filesystem is read-only

crash_segfault.cause: The process terminated outside Python control. Possible causes include a native library, driver, corrupt file or insufficient memory.
crash_segfault.consequences: Enigma2 may restart immediately without a readable Python error.
crash_segfault.restart: Perform a full receiver restart after updating the affected component.
steps: Identify the action performed immediately before the crash.
Update the image and the plug-in using native libraries.
Check available RAM and receiver temperature.
If the crash is repeatable, send the complete crashlog to the image or plug-in developer.
crash_segfault.title: System component segmentation fault

crash_skin.cause: The skin refers to a missing component, graphic, font or incompatible widget.
crash_skin.consequences: Enigma2 may display a crash screen whenever a particular menu is opened.
crash_skin.restart: Restart the GUI after changing the skin.
steps: Select the default skin supplied with the image.
Restart the GUI.
Update or remove the skin named in the crashlog.
If the error affects only one plug-in, check that plug-in's own skin.xml file.
crash_skin.title: Enigma2 skin error

crash_ssl.cause: The system date, certificate store or SSL libraries are invalid.
crash_ssl.consequences: Downloads over HTTPS may be blocked.
steps: Check the system date and time.
Update the CA certificate package from the image feed.
Do not disable SSL verification as a permanent workaround.
Check whether the issue affects many servers or only one.
crash_ssl.title: HTTPS certificate verification error

diagnostic_error.cause: E2 Doctor could not perform one of its tests on this image or receiver model.
diagnostic_error.consequences: That test result is incomplete, but the remaining modules may still work correctly.
steps: Save an E2 Doctor report.
Review the technical error details.
Send the report, receiver model and image version to the plug-in developer.
Do not make manual system changes based only on this message.
diagnostic_error.title: A diagnostic module ended with an error

dns_error.cause: The receiver cannot resolve a domain name to an IP address.
dns_error.consequences: GitHub, OPKG feeds and other internet services may be unavailable even though the network interface is connected.
steps: Check that the receiver received an IP address, gateway and DNS servers.
Check the network cable or Wi-Fi connection.
Run gateway, DNS and HTTPS tests.
If necessary, configure the router DNS or a public DNS server in the network settings.
dns_error.title: DNS name resolution is not working

epg_small.cause: EPG data has not been downloaded yet, the import was interrupted or the file location is incorrect.
epg_small.consequences: Programme descriptions may not be displayed.
steps: Check the EPG storage path in the image settings.
Run an EPG import manually and review its log.
Make sure the target storage device is mounted and writable.
Avoid storing a very large EPG file in flash on receivers with limited space.
epg_small.title: The EPG file is missing or unusually small

flash_critical.cause: The system partition is almost full.
flash_critical.consequences: Installing packages, saving settings and creating logs may fail and Enigma2 may become unstable.
flash_critical.restart: Restart the GUI after freeing space.
steps: Do not install or update packages until more space is available.
Use E2 Doctor to find the largest files.
Remove old crashlogs and temporary OPKG files.
Uninstall unused plug-ins and move EPG or picons to external storage.
flash_critical.title: Critically low free flash space

flash_low.cause: The amount of free space on the system partition is below the recommended level.
flash_low.consequences: Future updates and plug-in data may no longer fit in flash.
steps: Check which files and directories use the most space.
Remove old crashlogs and unnecessary downloaded IPK or archive files.
Uninstall plug-ins you no longer use.
Move EPG and picons to a mounted external device when supported by the image.
flash_low.title: Low free flash space

flash_read_error.cause: E2 Doctor could not read filesystem statistics for the root partition.
flash_read_error.consequences: The free-space result is unavailable and a filesystem or compatibility issue may exist.
steps: Check df -h and mount from a terminal.
Save an E2 Doctor report.
Check kernel messages for storage errors.
Do not format or repair the active root filesystem while Enigma2 is running.
flash_read_error.title: Flash filesystem information could not be read

https_error.cause: DNS works, but an HTTPS connection could not be established.
https_error.consequences: Updates and plug-ins using secure web connections may fail.
steps: Check the system date and time.
Run the extended network test.
Check CA certificates and SSL packages installed in the image.
Check whether the router or firewall blocks outbound HTTPS connections.
https_error.title: HTTPS connection failed

mount_readonly.cause: One or more mounted devices are available only in read-only mode.
mount_readonly.consequences: Recordings, timeshift, EPG and backups cannot be written to the affected device.
mount_readonly.restart: Restart the receiver after safely checking and repairing the filesystem.
steps: Identify the affected mount point.
Back up important data if the device can still be read.
Unmount the filesystem before running a repair tool.
If I/O errors are present, consider replacing the storage device.
mount_readonly.title: A storage device is mounted read-only

opkg_broken.cause: The OPKG database contains packages that are not fully installed or configured.
opkg_broken.consequences: Future package installations and updates may fail or leave the system inconsistent.
steps: Do not perform a full image update until the package state is understood.
Review the packages listed in the technical details.
Check free flash space and the OPKG installation log.
Reinstall or remove only the affected packages using commands appropriate for the installed image.
opkg_broken.title: Incomplete OPKG package state detected

opkg_db_missing.cause: The installed-package database is missing, empty or unreadable.
opkg_db_missing.consequences: OPKG may not know which packages are installed and package management may be unsafe.
steps: Check /var/lib/opkg/status and the OPKG configuration directories.
Do not copy a package database from another receiver or image.
Save your settings and verify the image filesystem.
If system package files are broadly damaged, reflash the correct image.
opkg_db_missing.title: The OPKG database is missing or unreadable

opkg_lock.cause: An OPKG lock file remained after an interrupted installation or update.
opkg_lock.consequences: Package operations may report that the package manager is busy.
steps: Make sure no installation or update is currently running.
Remove only an inactive lock file.
Run the OPKG operation again.
If the lock returns, inspect the installation log and free flash space.
opkg_lock.title: An inactive OPKG lock remains

opkg_missing.cause: The OPKG executable was removed, damaged or is stored in a non-standard location by the image.
opkg_missing.consequences: IPK packages cannot be installed, updated or removed correctly.
steps: Check whether /usr/bin/opkg exists.
Do not copy a random OPKG binary from another image.
Restore OPKG only from a package intended for the exact image in use.
If many system files are missing, consider reflashing the image.
opkg_missing.title: The OPKG package manager was not found

oscam_stopped.cause: The softcam was not started, stopped with an error or is not selected in the image softcam panel.
oscam_stopped.consequences: Services requiring a running softcam will not be handled.
steps: Check that OSCam is selected as the active softcam.
Check execute permission on the OSCam binary.
Review the latest OSCam log entries without publishing credentials.
Restart OSCam.
oscam_stopped.title: OSCam configuration exists, but the process is not running

python3_required.cause: The installed Enigma2 image uses a different major Python version than this package requires.
python3_required.consequences: The plug-in may fail to start or produce import and syntax errors.
python3_required.restart: Restart the GUI after installing a compatible package.
steps: Check the Python version with python3 --version.
Install an E2 Doctor build intended for the Python version used by the image.
Do not manually replace Python libraries inside the Enigma2 image.
python3_required.title: Python 3 environment required

ram_critical.cause: Memory available to new processes is almost exhausted and free swap space is too low.
ram_critical.consequences: Enigma2 may be terminated by the system, freeze or create a crashlog.
ram_critical.restart: A GUI restart is recommended; perform a full receiver restart if the problem returns.
steps: Check which processes use the most RAM.
Do not start additional demanding plug-ins.
Restart the Enigma2 GUI.
If available memory drops quickly again, update or remove the plug-in causing the memory leak.
ram_critical.title: Critically low available RAM

ram_low.cause: One or more services use a large amount of memory or the GUI has been running for a long time.
ram_low.consequences: Menus may respond slowly and demanding plug-ins may close or crash.
ram_low.restart: First restart the GUI; a full receiver restart is only necessary if the problem returns.
steps: Check which processes use the most memory.
Close unused plug-ins and media players.
If the issue started after opening a particular plug-in, update or remove it.
Restart the GUI and check whether available memory increased.
ram_low.title: Low available RAM

storage_warning.cause: The kernel log or mount information indicates a write error, unsafe removal, read-only mode or low free space.
storage_warning.consequences: Recordings, timeshift, EPG or backups may not be saved and continued use may worsen filesystem damage.
storage_warning.restart: Perform a full receiver restart after repairing or remounting the device.
steps: Do not disconnect the storage device while data is being written.
Identify the affected device: {errors}.
Back up important recordings and files.
Repair the filesystem only after unmounting it, preferably on a computer or in service mode.
If I/O errors appear, consider replacing the storage device.
storage_warning.title: Storage or filesystem warning

system_load_high.cause: The processor has been heavily loaded for longer than expected. A stuck plug-in, scan, update, transcoding task or storage problem may be responsible.
system_load_high.consequences: Menus may respond slowly, video may stutter and plug-ins may stop responding.
system_load_high.restart: Restart the GUI only if high load remains without a clear reason.
steps: Wait a few minutes and run the scan again.
Open Safe Tools and check the processes using the most RAM and CPU.
Close running plug-ins, channel scans and demanding streams.
If the load does not fall, restart the GUI and then the receiver if necessary.
system_load_high.title: High system load

temperature_critical.cause: The receiver is not being cooled correctly or the sensor reports a dangerous temperature.
temperature_critical.consequences: The receiver may freeze, shut down or be damaged.
temperature_critical.restart: Switch off the receiver and allow it to cool before starting it again.
steps: Stop demanding operations and switch off the receiver.
Check that ventilation openings are clear and the fan works, if fitted.
After cooling, place the receiver in a well-ventilated location and start it again.
If the temperature quickly rises again, the hardware requires technical inspection.
temperature_critical.title: Critical receiver temperature

temperature_high.cause: Ventilation is restricted, the receiver is placed in a closed cabinet or the processor remains heavily loaded.
temperature_high.consequences: Slowdowns, instability and reduced component life may result.
steps: Leave free space around all ventilation openings.
Remove dust from the enclosure without opening a powered device.
Do not place the receiver on another heat-producing device.
Check the temperature again after several minutes.
temperature_high.title: High receiver temperature

time_invalid.cause: The receiver did not synchronise its clock over the internet or the time service is not working.
time_invalid.consequences: HTTPS, EPG, certificate and update errors may occur.
time_invalid.restart: A GUI restart is normally not required.
steps: Check the network connection and configured time zone.
Restart chronyd or ntpd.
After synchronisation, check the current date with the date command.
If the clock resets after disconnecting power, check the image and hardware settings.
time_invalid.title: Invalid system date or time

tuner_hardware_missing.cause: The tuner driver did not provide data in /proc/bus/nim_sockets or the image uses a different diagnostic interface.
tuner_hardware_missing.consequences: E2 Doctor cannot confirm the number and type of installed tuners.
steps: Check in the image menus whether the tuners are visible.
Perform a full receiver restart.
If the tuners are still missing, verify that the image and drivers support the receiver model.
Do not change tuner configuration until the system detects the hardware.
tuner_hardware_missing.title: The tuner list cannot be read

tuner_missing.cause: No config.Nims entries exist in the settings file or tuner settings have not been saved yet.
tuner_missing.consequences: Satellite, cable or terrestrial scanning may not work.
steps: Open Menu → Setup → Service searching → Tuner configuration.
Configure each tuner according to the connected antenna installation.
Save the settings and run E2 Doctor again.
Do not copy tuner settings from a different receiver model.
tuner_missing.title: No tuner configuration was found

tuner_no_lock.cause: No signal may be caused by the antenna system, DiSEqC/Unicable configuration, an incorrect frequency or a temporarily unavailable transponder.
tuner_no_lock.consequences: The channel will not display video or may report that no data is available.
steps: Check whether the issue affects one channel or every channel from the same satellite.
Check the antenna cable and connectors.
Compare the tuner configuration with the installed DiSEqC or Unicable system.
Test another known active transponder.
Do not automatically change tuner settings without first backing up the configuration.
tuner_no_lock.title: The active tuner did not obtain LOCK

tuner_xml_missing.cause: Tuner settings exist, but satellites.xml, cables.xml or terrestrial.xml could not be found.
tuner_xml_missing.consequences: Manual or automatic scanning may not show available positions and frequencies.
tuner_xml_missing.restart: Restart the GUI after installing the XML files.
steps: Install a current satellites.xml file or the appropriate scan-data package.
Check the /etc/tuxbox directory.
Use a file intended for Enigma2 and do not change tuner settings unnecessarily.
Restart the GUI after replacing the file.
tuner_xml_missing.title: Scanning data files are missing
